title: iochroma edule
intro: Iochroma edule, also known as the "forest firecracker" or "tree toadstool," is a shrub-like plant native to the Andean region of South America. This plant contains alkaloids with reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Iochroma edule are believed to include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent infections. Iochroma edule can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in the form of a poultice or topical application.
